events.title: San Francisco St. Patrick's Day Pub Crawl: LepraCon
events.description_preview: San Francisco's Biggest St. Patrick's Day Party Every March, San Francisco turns a little greener, a...
events.description_detail:
San Francisco's Biggest St. Patrick's Day Party
Every March, San Francisco turns a little greener, a little louder & a lot more fun. November and December are filled with friends, family, presents and usually a little time off of work, but the beginning of the year is always a little slow as you get back into your routine. However, as March gets closer, you can feel the excitement brewing as all the upcoming concerts, festival lineups and special events get announced. St. Patrick's Day is the day that kickstarts the fun stuff for the year.
For more than a decade, the city’s St. Patrick’s Day Pub Crawl has been the official start to the fun of the new year, bringing thousands of people into the streets, bars and neighborhoods that know how to throw a proper party. For 2026, Saturday, March 14 is the day it all comes together.
Produced by CrawlSF, San Francisco’s original pub crawl company, the San Francisco St. Patrick’s Day Pub Crawl is the biggest St. Patrick’s Day party in the city. Over 10,000 people will flood The Marina, Nob Hill, Russian Hill, Polk Gulch and more for a full day of bar hopping, music and cheap drinks. It’s the kind of event that feels less like a bar crawl and more like a moving festival that heads to one neighborhood at a time.
Saturday is the heart of the weekend. Starting in the afternoon and rolling well into the night, the crawl brings together party people who treat this event as a yearly tradition. Bars open their doors early, DJs turn things up and the streets fill with green outfits, Irish cheer and that rare San Francisco energy where everyone feels like they’re part of the same plan.
What makes this crawl special isn’t just the size (although it’s massive). It’s the drinks, djs bars and, most importantly, all the awesome and fun people who attend CrawlSf events. Participants check in, grab their wristbands and maps & then move freely between some of the city’s best neighborhoods.

events.title: Official St. Patrick's Day San Francisco Bar Crawl 2026
events.description_preview: Official St. Patrick's Day San Francisco Bar Crawl 2026 Welcome to St. Patrick’s Day SF 2026, the ul...
events.description_detail:
Official St. Patrick's Day San Francisco Bar Crawl 2026
Welcome to St. Patrick’s Day SF 2026, the ultimate celebration! ☘️ This year, thousands of party-goers, leprechauns, and lucky bar crawlers from all over the city will gather for an unforgettable event. Join us in the lively Mission District, where the official St. Patrick’s Day bar crawl will take over San Francisco’s best bars and nightclubs.
On Saturday, March 14th, the Mission will be bursting with spirit as the streets fill with green from bar to bar. From classic shamrock fits and leprechaun suits to creative, outrageous, and stylish green looks — everyone is welcome to bring their own flavor to this legendary St. Patrick’s Day party in SF.
Participating venues will offer a stacked lineup of craft cocktails, St. Patrick’s Day inspired specials, green drinks, and festive surprises to keep the party going all day and night. Enjoy festive cocktails, green beer, high-energy dance floors, and nonstop music as DJs set the tone for one of the biggest St. Patrick’s Day Pub Crawls in SF.
As we celebrate, let’s make safety a priority. Please drink responsibly, stay hydrated, and respect your fellow bar crawlers and the local community. Let’s make this a memorable and spirited event for everyone.
Mark your calendars and get ready to explore the Mission, enjoy incredible drinks, and celebrate with thousands of others dressed in green at the biggest St. Patrick’s Day bar crawl in San Francisco. Let’s get lucky. ☘️
🚨 FREE ENTRY TO 7+ FESTIVE BARS & NIGHTCLUBS!
🕺LIVE DJS AND ENTERTAINMENT AT SELECT VENUES!
☘️ JOIN THE LUCKIEST ST. PATRICK'S DAY BAR CRAWL IN SF!
🍻 EXCLUSIVE HOLIDAY DRINK SPECIALS!
Participating venues include:
Soundtrack...and more to be announced!
Access to all participating bars is limited to those with StPatricksDaySF.com event wristbands, and the list of venues may be updated.
Official St. Patrick's Day San Francisco Bar Crawl 2026
Welcome to St. Patrick’s Day SF 2026, the ultimate celebration!

events.title: Agave Girls at Copita hosted by Joanne Weir - Women's History Month Edition
events.description_preview: Join us in Sausalito as we celebrate women together with great food, fun friends, and tequila from V...
events.description_detail:
Join us in Sausalito as we celebrate women together with great food, fun friends, and tequila from Volans on Tuesday, March10!
Join us for a very special evening as we gather together to celebrate Women's History Month at Copita in Sausalito on Tuesday, March 10. The festivities will be led by the original Agave Girl herself, Joanne Weir, star of the PBS program "Joanne Weir's Plates and Places" and author of Tequila: A Guide to Types, Flights, Cocktails and Bites.
Agave Girls promises to be another fun, informative and entertaining evening of tequila and cocktail tasting, delicious food and wonderful camaraderie. Enjoy passed appetizers, a tasting flight of tequila, and a three-course sit-down dinner paired with tequila cocktails featuring the wonderful award-winning tequilas of Volans. As in the past, we'll start with a cocktail reception and a chance to mingle with Joanne and other like-minded women. Then we'll enjoy a dinner featuring Copita Sausalito Executive Chef Candy Diaz's culinary wonders paired with Volans' ultra-premium tequilas.
Volans is a family-owned tequila made at El Pandillo distillery in Los Altos de Jalisco, Mexico. What makes Volans truly unique is the blend of three distinct water sources used in production: deep well water from over 450 feet below ground, spring water from an artesian source high in the mountains, and rainwater collected during summer months. This gentle mix of mineral-rich and soft waters creates Volans' signature flavor profile. Crafted using a traditional 100% mechanical tahona process by master distiller Felipe Camarena, each batch honors centuries-old methods while delivering exceptional quality and taste.
The doors open at 6pm and the seated dinner starts at 6:30pm. The cost for this event is $125 per person and includes tax, gratuity and Eventbrite fees. Tickets must be purchased in advance.

events.title: SF HOLI SUNDOWNER (Rooftop Bollywood Party)
events.description_preview: Get ready to paint the town on March 14th at our Holi Sundowner Rooftop Party! Get ready to paint th...
events.description_detail:
Get ready to paint the town on March 14th at our Holi Sundowner Rooftop Party!
Get ready to paint the town on March 14th at our Holi Sundowner Rooftop Party!
Celebrate the festival of colors in true style at the BEI Hotel rooftop in San Francisco from 5:00 PM to 9:30 PM. We’re bringing you DJ Brown Buoy with a vibrant mix of Bollywood, Bhangra, and Indo House beats that’ll keep you dancing as the sun goes down.
Why You Can’t Miss This:
• Rooftop Vibes: Enjoy spectacular city views while you immerse yourself in the spirit of Holi.
• Free Colors Provided: We’ll supply organic colors so you can splash and play without worry.
• Dress Code: Trendy White – Come dressed in white and leave drenched in every color of happiness.
• Music & More: DJ Brown Buoy will spin high-energy tracks, blending traditional and modern tunes. Appetizers available for purchase.
• 21+ Only: Bring your friends (and ID!) for an unforgettable evening of color, music, and rooftop fun.
Secure Your Spot Now!
You don’t want to miss San Francisco’s most colorful rooftop party. Book your ticket today and experience Holi like never before—up high in the skyline, showered in color, and surrounded by incredible beats. Early bird tickets will sell out fast!
Let’s paint the sky with color and celebration!
VENUE: Hotel Bei (Rooftop), 50 8th St, San Francisco, CA 94103
$20 PARKING AVAILABLE AT THE VENUE (until 11pm)
Overnight Parking: $40
** SATURDAY MARCH 14th! **
TO RESERVE BOTTLE SERVICE - TEXT OR CALL 323-482-7070
OR FOR ANY OTHER BOTTLE PRICING & QUESTIONS, PLEASE EMAIL US AT desibeatzsf@gmail.com
*********************************************************************************
Event Photography & Videography Disclaimer
By attending this event, you acknowledge and agree that Desibeatz and its affiliates may capture photos and videos during the event. These materials may be used for promotional, marketing, and social media purposes without any further consent or compensation.

events.title: San Jose St. Patrick's Day Weekend Bar Crawl 2026
events.location: 73 North San Pedro Street, San Jose, CA 95110
events.description_preview: The Official St Patrick's Day Bar Crawl of 2026! The Official St Patrick's Day Bar Crawl of 2026! Ge...
events.description_detail:
The Official St Patrick's Day Bar Crawl of 2026!
The Official St Patrick's Day Bar Crawl of 2026!
Get ready for the San Jose St. Patrick's Day Weekend Bar Crawl on Saturday, March 14th, 2026! Kick off St. Patrick’s Day weekend in San Jose in style by hitting the city’s top bars! Enjoy St. Paddy's drink specials and soak up the festive energy with your crew. This is the must-attend St. Patrick’s Day event of 2026!
Whether you're Irish at heart or just love an epic party, this bar crawl is your ticket to an unforgettable celebration.Explore the best nightlife spots, enjoy St. Paddy’s vibes, and join hundreds of partygoers rocking their greenest outfits!
Your ticket to the San Jose St. Patrick’s Day Bar Crawl includes drinks, free entry, drink specials, and a free after party as you bar hop through the best bars in San Jose.
The Official San Jose St. Patrick’s Day Weekend Bar Crawl brings together locals and visitors for a high-energy celebration featuring multiple bars, included drinks, exclusive specials, and a free after party — all in one walkable experience. This San Jose St. Patrick’s Day Bar Crawl is one of the most popular St. Patrick’s Day events in San Jose, CA.
📍 Location: The Old Wagon Saloon & Grill📆 Date: Saturday, March 14th 2026🕒 Time: 4:00 pm - 10:00 pm
Check-in begins at The Old Wagon Saloon & Grill between 4:00pm and 8:00pm, where you will receive a wristband granting access to participating bars and drink specials.
Looking for more events in the area? Explore all upcoming San Jose bar crawls happening throughout the year.
📧 Contact us: Our 24/7 support team is here to assist!Email info@pubcrawls.com for ticket questions, event details, or anything else.
🎟️ Don’t miss this year’s best St. Patrick's Day Bar Crawl! Secure your spot now and make St. Patrick’s Day weekend 2026 unforgettable! 🍀🔥
Disclaimer: Must be 21 years or older to attend with a valid form of government-issued ID. All participating bars and specials may be subject to change.

events.title: Pocket Gamer 20th Anniversary Party San Francisco 2026
events.description_preview: Join us in San Francisco for a special edition of our PG Party during GDC week! Relax and enjoy a dr...
events.description_detail:
Join us in San Francisco for a special edition of our PG Party during GDC week! Relax and enjoy a drink with games industry professionals
Kick off those business connections during the awesome GDC week (when we're in town for our reworked & streamlined mobile games industry event, Pocket Gamer Connects Summit San Francisco 2026 [registered attendees get early access to our party!]) by attending another excellent Pocket Gamer Party.
All games industry professionals are welcome at 715 Harrison nightclub on the evening of Monday, March 9, for informal networking, drinks, and fun times.N.B. 1: PG Connects Summit San Francisco ticket holders DO NOT to register (PGC ticket includes PG Party access)
N.B. 2: Please note that a ticket does not guarantee access to the event and that the free bar is limited – access will be granted on a first-come, first served basis and you may need to queue.
March 9, the first evening of GDC week and the first night of PG Connects Summit San Francisco 2026. The party kicks off for all at 8:30pm.
715 Harrison is a full-feature nightclub and event space (it's also the venue of PG Connects Summit San Francisco 2026) with varied music and dynamic decor. It's about 7 minutes' walk southeast of Moscone.

events.title: 2026 Indie Arcade Party with: Unity, VIVERSE & Genies
events.description_preview: Experience the largest Indie Arcade Party during the week of GDC 2026 as an official GDC affiliate e...
events.description_detail:
Experience the largest Indie Arcade Party during the week of GDC 2026 as an official GDC affiliate event
Experience the largest Indie Arcade Party during the week of GDC 2026! As an official GDC Affiliate Event, SF Game Development invites you to our 5th annual night of game industry networking and indie game showcases. Join over a thousand developers at the iconic DNA Lounge in San Francisco on Tuesday, March 10th. Proudly hosted alongside Unity, VIVERSE, and Genies, this is the FREE GDC party you can't miss. RSVP now on Meetup to secure your spot for the ultimate San Francisco game dev celebration! Also, if you want to bring a friend, just send them the event link here and have them register themselves (we need an accurate guest count per GDC guidelines).
🔥 Four massive rooms packed with game demos, tech-talks and music🎲 35+ indie game demos from members of the SF Game Development community🎤 8 tech talks featuring industry-leading companies & speakers🎵 Video game-themed music in The Lounge for dancing
Whether you're a developer, publisher, artist, student, indie creator, journalist, or just love games, this is the perfect place to expand your career and learn about new games and solutions coming to market.
🚀 No GDC pass? No problem! This event is open to the public (18+)🎥 Want a taste of last year’s event? Watch the recap video!🔗 Having trouble registering on Meetup? No worries—sign up on Luma instead.
🕢 7:30 PM – Unity | Corrie Green, Senior Developer Advocate🎙️Performance Analysis and Solutions in Unity 6 🎙️This session explores common performance pitfalls encountered during development, and shows how to isolate them using Unity 6’s suite of analysis tools—including the Profiler, Memory Profiler, Project Auditor, and build analysis tools—and demos comparisons with best practices to improve scene performance.

events.title: Silent Book Club Oakland
events.description_preview: It’s Introvert’s Happy Hour, y’all! Bring a book, grab a beverage, and read with us for Silent Readi...
events.description_detail:
It’s Introvert’s Happy Hour, y’all! Bring a book, grab a beverage, and read with us for Silent Reading Hour.
Silent Book Club is a global community of readers with chapters in 54 countries -- welcome to the Oakland chapter of Silent Book Club! We are super excited to host you all -- whether you've been reading for decades or just for a month, bring a book and join us at the reading table. Come with a friend or alone -- you are welcome as you are!
For our weekday meet-ups: Pack a book, a book light, and extra layers, and head over to Temescal Brewing for our meet-ups every 2nd Tuesday of the month. We meet at the reserved back tables in the outdoor patio.
Temescal Brewing serves craft beer with bar snacks and they usually have a food truck parked adjacent to their outdoor patio. Their outdoor patio is ADA accessible.
Follow us on Instagram for updates or feel free to email us [silentbookcluboakland(at)gmail.com] if you have any questions.

events.description_detail:
Boost your glow and confidence with Ultherapy – a non-invasive collagen building treatment lasting up to a year.
Boost your glow and confidence with Ultherapy – a non-invasive collagen building treatment lasting up to a year.
There’s a reason Ultherapy is the go-to treatment for celebrities before major red carpet events. 📸 While the long-term collagen building takes a few months, patients often see an initial tightening of the skin immediately following the procedure.
Whether you have a gala, a wedding, or just want to stay ahead of the aging curve, Ultherapy provides a lifted look with zero time off work. It’s the perfect non-invasive solution for budiling collagen and skin tightening.
Join us at Cheng Plastic Surgery in Saratoga and chat with our offical Ultherapy Prime brand partner and nurse staff at Cheng. We'll be answering all your questions on youth preserving longevity.
By RSVP'ing to the event you'll also enjoy exclusive one day promotinal offers on Ultherapy, Botox, and Epicutis skincare.
12945 Saratoga Avenue

events.title: REBECCA ROBINSON at Books Inc. Pruneyard
events.description_preview: Join returning author Rebecca Robinson at Books Inc. Pruneyard for the release of her latest book, T...
events.description_detail:
Join returning author Rebecca Robinson at Books Inc. Pruneyard for the release of her latest book, The Wicked and the Damned!
In the exhilarating second romantasy novel in Rebecca Robinson’s Dark Inheritance Trilogy, a fiery couple forced apart must navigate a deadly maze of politics and power to reunite across a continent at war.
Torn from her husband Reid’s arms and dragged back to her homeland in chains, Vaasa is no longer a ruler, but a political pawn. Now under the control of Ozik—a cunning Zetyr witch with a stranglehold on her magic—she faces annulment, forced betrothal, and a kingdom that no longer feels like her own. As Ozik’s machinations aim to install himself as the true power behind the Asteryan throne, Vaasa is forced to take part in his game. Meanwhile, across the continent, Reid is done playing politics. But waging war is never simple, and a soldier’s fury cannot stand up against Icruria’s bureaucracy. With allies fading and time slipping away, Reid may have no choice but to infiltrate Asterya on his own to find the woman he loves and bring her home. As Vaasa’s magic begins to flare, she desperately seeks control, even if Ozik is the only one who can offer it. She then finds a cryptic final message from her mother about a precious missing necklace that might just be the answer to finally freeing her magic from Ozik. Yet when a ghost from Vaasa’s past reemerges among the suitors vying for her hand, escape might be within reach—but will it demand a betrayal that real love may not survive?
By day, Rebecca Robinson works as a high school administrator. By night, she’s an avid reader, writer, and consumer of all things art. When she’s not writing, Rebecca loves to cook, spend time with her husband and son, and lounge in the garden with her husky.
Join returning author Rebecca Robinson at Books Inc. Pruneyard for the release of her latest book, The Wicked and the Damned!

events.title: All Things Meetings San Francisco- Connection Reception
events.location: Pier 3, Hornblower Landing, San Francisco, CA 95111
events.description_preview: Ready to mingle and make some new connections at the All Things Meetings San Francisco- Connection R...
events.description_detail:
Ready to mingle and make some new connections at the All Things Meetings San Francisco- Connection Reception?
All Things Meetings Connection Reception
We invite you to join us for an in-person dockside event aboard the City Cruises San Francisco Belle in San Francisco, CA.
A Bay Area landmark, the San Francisco Belle boasts the title of the largest dining vessel on the West Coast. Her wrap-around decks, paddle wheel and gold detailing recall an old-world elegance. She boasts three spacious interior decks, panoramic views of the city skyline from the 50+ windows and an expansive sundeck. Her presence on the bay will capture your imagination with a style that is uniquely San Francisco.
This event is specifically designed for professionals responsible for planning corporate, associations, and social meetings and events. It's a fantastic, one-stop opportunity to network and connect directly with key event industry suppliers and resources.
Register now for an evening that promises to be both fun and highly productive!

events.title: Alex & Maya Valdivia's Measure the Silence Album Release Concert
events.description_preview: Join us for an unforgettable night of music as Alex & Maya Valdivia's Measure the Silence Album Rele...
events.description_detail:
Join us for an unforgettable night of music as Alex & Maya Valdivia's Measure the Silence Album Release Concert takes over the stage!
Measure The Silence is a full album live performance by Alex & Maya Valdivia, marking the release of a record about the things we don’t say and the weight they carry.
The album explores the emotional spaces between people: silence, restraint, resentment, memory, longing, and the fragile ties that hold relationships together or pull them apart. Across 14 songs, it traces what happens when feelings are postponed, when futures are abandoned, when truth hums just under the surface.
• Acoustic-led folk / trova / rock
• Layered harmonies, canons, and choral moments
• Subtle electronic textures alongside organic instruments
• A quiet intense, immersive listening experience
The performance also includes a visual collaboration with Fuse Theatre, using masks, movement, and projections to embody the album’s themes, silence, identity, fracture, and connection. Some of the masks used onstage are created by members of the audience in advance, blurring the line between witness and participant.
If you’re drawn to artists like Damien Rice, The Swell Season, Jorge Drexler, or modern folk that values restraint, tension, and emotional honesty, this night is for you.
Join us for an unforgettable night of music as Alex & Maya Valdivia's Measure the Silence Album Release Concert takes over the stage!
Measure The Silence is a full album live performance by Alex & Maya Valdivia, marking the release of a record about the things we don’t say and the weight they carry.
The album explores the emotional spaces between people: silence, restraint, resentment, memory, longing, and the fragile ties that hold relationships together or pull them apart. Across 14 songs, it traces what happens when feelings are postponed, when futures are abandoned, when truth hums just under the surface.

San Mateo County is an innovative and invigorating place to work and offers many exciting opportunities for both classified and certificated education professionals! The Education Employment Recruitment Fair brings together well-qualified candidates seeking employment with our county's twenty-three school districts and county office of education student programs, all eager to hire highly skilled and dedicated individuals to join their teams.
Whether you are pursuing a classified position, seeking a teaching position, interested in credentialing information, or exploring opportunities in administration, join us at the Education Employment Recruitment Fair to find your next opportunity.

events.title: MAXINE SHARF — MAXI’S KITCHEN BOOK TOUR
events.description_preview: The wildly popular culinary creator brings her viral, confidence-boosting approach from the screen t...
events.description_detail:
The wildly popular culinary creator brings her viral, confidence-boosting approach from the screen to the page in her debut cookbook
Join us for a conversation and meet and greet with beloved culinary creator Maxine Sharf celebrating the release of her debut cookbook, Maxi’s Kitchen: Easy Go-To Recipes to Make Again and Again. With 4 million+ followers across social platforms, Maxine is known for her unfussy, deeply comforting recipes – and for the joyful, often hilarious cooking moments she shares alongside her mom, Ann Sharf.
Warm, joyful, and irresistibly practical, Maxi’s Kitchen is built for real life – the kind where dinner needs to happen whether it’s a rushed Monday night or a lingering Sunday morning. Organized by day of the week, the book meets you where you are: quick, no-stress meals early on, playful dishes midweek, and indulgent cooking projects for the weekend. This is food that feels personal, doable, and meant to be made again and again.
After being laid off from a decade-long career in tech sales, Maxine turned to cooking full time, building a global community rooted in confidence and connection in the kitchen. Her food reflects a richly layered heritage – Korean, Chinese, Romanian, Russian, and Polish – paired with a California sensibility that values ease, freshness, and flavor.
In this conversation, Maxine will share the story behind Maxi’s Kitchen, from family recipes and cultural memory to designing a cookbook organized by mood and day of the week – with plenty of inspiration for what to cook tonight, tomorrow, and beyond.
Whether you’re a longtime follower or discovering Maxine for the first time, this is a celebration of food as comfort, connection, and daily joy.Our partner for this program is Omnivore Books
Questions?

events.title: Annual Post-Parade Party—St. Patrick's Day Weekend
events.description_preview: Head back to the Irish Center after the parade (look for the UICC float) and enjoy hot food and plen...
events.description_detail:
Head back to the Irish Center after the parade (look for the UICC float) and enjoy hot food and plenty of Irish entertainment!
Join us at the annual Corned Beef and Cabbage meal, featuring San Francisco’s own Robert’s Corned Beef, and know you are coming to the biggest, best, post-parade St. Patrick’s Day party in San Francisco!
There will be live bands, Irish Dancing, and the Irish Pipers in three locations (two indoors, one outdoors) throughout our facility. Additional food items such as the popular corned beef sliders and grilled cheese and hot dogs for the kids as well plus plenty of Irish treats and sweets at our Irish Shoppe (2-6 pm), which will also have fresh soda and brown bread.
The Dowling Library will be open from 2-5 pm, so check out the latest titles and offerings—and bring the kids for stickers and coloring sheets!
Current UICC members and all youth under 18 are free; $10 cover for adult non-members. You must show proof of age 21+ to purchase alcohol.

events.title: Hands-On: Northern Egg Pasta Traditions
events.location: 2855 Stevens Creek Boulevard, San Jose, CA 95128
events.description_preview: It’s time to get your hands messy! In our hands-on format, our Scuola Chef will guide you through ho...
events.description_detail:
It’s time to get your hands messy! In our hands-on format, our Scuola Chef will guide you through how to prepare Northern Egg Pasta.
It’s time to get your hands messy! In our hands-on format, our Scuola Chef will prepare the signature dish for you to taste in class. You’ll then dive right in to making the recipe yourself from scratch. Take your culinary creation with you at the end of class and enjoy your own hard work at home!
Fresh Egg Pasta Shaping
Egg pasta is the prized pasta type that is enjoyed throughout Northern and Central Italy. Similarly to dried pasta, fresh egg pasta comes from two humble ingredients: unbleached all-purpose flour and, instead of water, eggs. Shaping the dough is often done by hand on a wooden surface or with a hand-crank machine. In this class, you’ll make your own egg pasta dough from scratch.
In this Class You Will:

events.title: Monteraponi Wine Dinner
events.description_preview: A dinner featuring Monteraponi wines paired with a multi-course menu. The history of Monteraponi dat...
events.description_detail:
A dinner featuring Monteraponi wines paired with a multi-course menu.
The history of Monteraponi dates back to the 10th century, when the estate served as a fortified settlement for Count Ugo, Governor of Tuscany. After centuries of varied ownership, the property was acquired in 1974 by the Braganti family, who initially devoted their efforts to preserving the historic hamlet before turning their focus to viticulture.
Monteraponi’s modern reputation was shaped by Michele Braganti, who assumed leadership of the estate in 2003 with a vision to restore the “old school” soul of Radda in Chianti. Under his direction, the winery embraced strictly organic farming and minimalist cellar practices, establishing itself as a benchmark producer of pure, high-altitude Sangiovese. Today, Monteraponi’s wines are celebrated for their elegance, structure, and profound sense of place.
Please join us for an intimate evening featuring a curated selection of Monteraponi wines, thoughtfully paired with a seasonal multi-course menu. We look forward to sharing these expressive wines and the story behind them with you.
Pear and Arugula SaladPecorino Romano, Toasted WalnutsTrebbiano, Colli della Toscana Centrale 2023~CampanelleEnglish Peas, Guanciale, Foraged MushroomsChianti Classico 2023~Braised Lamb ShankPearl Onions, Baby Carrots, ParsnipsChianti Classico Riserva, Il Campitello 2021Super-Tuscan Sangiovese, Baron`Ugo, Toscana Rosso 2019~Bittersweet Chocolate TartBurnt Caramel, Cocoa Nibs, Candied HazelnutsChianti Classico Gran Selezione, Bragantino 2019Chianti Classico Gran Selezione, Bragantino 2020
*Advance notice is required for food allergy-related menu modifications. As soon as you purchase ticket(s), please forward the email confirmation with details to info@augustinerestaurant.com.

Join us at the table and watch our Scuola Chef prepare focaccia right in front of you, step-by-step.
In this Demo & Dine format, our Scuola Chef will prepare and show the steps it takes to prepare  focaccia dough from scratch. You’ll then dive right in learning how to stretch your own focaccia dough, how to make a salamoia, and top your own focaccia to be enjoyed!  Take your culinary creation with you at the end of class and enjoy your own hard work at home!
Focaccia is a chewy and flat bread loaded with olive oil and other toppings. Its signature dimpled appearance is because of a technique used to prevent large bubbles on the surface. In this class, you’ll make your own focaccia dough from scratch.

events.title: Michael Pollan's A WORLD APPEARS At First Congregational Church of Berkeley
events.description_preview: The Bestselling Berkeley Author Presents His Latest Book, An Exploration Of Consciousness And A Medi...
events.description_detail:
The Bestselling Berkeley Author Presents His Latest Book, An Exploration Of Consciousness And A Meditation On The Essence Of Our Humanity
Join Mrs. Dalloway's Bookstore, in partnership with The Elmwood Business Association, on Tuesday, March 10 at 7:00 PM when we host international bestselling author Michael Pollan. Michael will be at the First Congregational Church of Berkeley to present his brand new book A World Appears: A Journey Into Consciousness.  Journalist and podcaster Maya Shankar will join Michael for the conversation.
Tickets include admission for one and one hardcover copy of A World Appears, pre-signed by Michael Pollan.
Books bundled with tickets may only be picked up at the venue the night of the event, and cannot be picked up in-store beforehand. Ticket holders who purchased a book-with-ticket and are unable to attend the event will be able to pick up their book at Mrs. Dalloway's Bookstore up to 30 days following the event.
From the #1 New York Times bestselling author of How to Change Your Mind, a panoptic exploration of consciousness—what it is, who has it, and why—and a meditation on the essence of our humanity.
When it comes to the phenomenon that is consciousness, there is one point on which scientists, philosophers, and artists all agree: it feels like something to be us. Yet the fact that we have subjective experience of the world remains one of nature’s greatest mysteries. How is it that our mental operations are accompanied by feelings, thoughts, and a sense of self? What would a scientific investigation of our inner life look like, when we have as little distance and perspective on it as fish do of the sea? In A World Appears, Michael Pollan traces the unmapped continent that is consciousness, bringing radically different perspectives—scientific, philosophical, literary, spiritual and psychedelic—to see what each can teach us about this central fact of life.

events.title: Whatever It Takes: Tackling the Housing Affordability Crisis
events.description_preview: Join the Terner Center & Labs on Thursday, March 12 to explore the affordability challenge from a ho...
events.description_detail:
Join the Terner Center & Labs on Thursday, March 12 to explore the affordability challenge from a housing perspective.
People across the country are experiencing an affordability crisis. About half of U.S. renters are cost-burdened, spending a third of their income on housing; more than a quarter spend half of their income or more. High housing costs constrain labor mobility, deepen racial and economic inequities, and erode household financial stability.
Join the Terner Center and Terner Labs on Thursday, March 12, for "Whatever it Takes: Tackling the Housing Affordability Crisis." Our panel will share innovations and evidence-backed approaches to make housing affordable. We will hear from:
The event also marks the 30th anniversary of the passing of our namesake Don Terner, who took a “whatever it takes” approach to the housing crisis. Throughout the conversation, we’ll draw on lessons learned from Don’s work driving California housing policy reform, launching a Bay Area affordable housing nonprofit, and educating future housing leaders at UC Berkeley.
The event will take place at the UC Berkeley Faculty Club, with a networking reception at 4:30pm. The program will begin at 5:30pm.
If you require an accommodation for effective communication (ASL interpreting/alternative media formats, etc.) or information about campus mobility access features in order to fully participate in this event, please contact Jessie Modlin at jmodlin@berkeley.edu by February 26.
